title: SelfCheckGPT: Zero-Resource Black-Box Hallucination Detection
abstract: We propose SelfCheckGPT, a sampling-based approach that can detect hallucinated responses from black-box language models without any external database. The key idea is that if a model has knowledge of a concept, sampled responses are likely to be consistent; hallucinated facts diverge across samples. We evaluate on the WikiBio dataset and show that SelfCheckGPT detects non-factual sentences and ranks passages by factuality.

title: DEER: A Benchmark for Deep Research Report Evaluation
abstract: DEER organizes an expert-developed taxonomy for evaluating deep research reports, spanning 7 dimensions and 25 subdimensions with 101 rubric items. It introduces a claim verification architecture that checks both cited and uncited claims against retrieved evidence.
